items.title: Bias
items.description: Bias² and variance trade off in the squared-error decomposition: lowering variance with simpler models often raises bias, and vice versa.

items.title: Model complexity
items.description: More flexible model classes typically increase Var(ŷ) on finite data while reducing bias; complexity is the main dial for the bias–variance balance.

items.title: Expectation and variance
items.description: Var(·) in the decomposition uses the same expectation and variance operators as Var(X) for random variables; here the randomness is over training sets D.

items.title: Cross-validation
items.description: K-fold CV estimates out-of-sample error that reflects both bias and variance; fold-to-fold score spread is a practical variance diagnostic.

items.title: Underfitting vs overfitting
items.description: Overfitting is the high-variance regime (training ≪ validation performance); underfitting is often high-bias with relatively low variance.

items.title: L1/L2 regularization
items.description: Weight penalties shrink effective model capacity to reduce Var(ŷ) at the cost of some bias— a standard variance-reduction lever.
